name: Wikipedia MCP Server
what_it_does: Wikipedia MCP server enabling AI agents to search and retrieve Wikipedia content — fetching article summaries, searching for topics, retrieving full article sections, and integrating Wikipedia's comprehensive encyclopedia into agent-driven knowledge retrieval and research workflows.
use_cases: Searching Wikipedia for factual information from research agents
Retrieving article summaries for quick fact-checking from analysis agents
Getting detailed information on topics from knowledge agents
Finding Wikipedia references for report generation from writing agents
Accessing structured encyclopedia data from education agents
Cross-referencing information with Wikipedia from fact-checking agents
not_for: Real-time news or current events (Wikipedia content may be days/weeks delayed)
Specialized technical documentation (general encyclopedia, not technical docs)
Authoritative scientific citations (Wikipedia not acceptable in academic papers)
best_when: An agent needs general factual knowledge retrieval — Wikipedia provides broad coverage of topics with reliable summaries for general knowledge questions.
avoid_when: You need real-time information, technical documentation, or academically citable sources.

auth.notes: No authentication required — Wikipedia API is publicly accessible without credentials. Community MCP.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Wikipedia API is completely free with rate limits
pricing.notes: Wikipedia API is free and open. MCP server is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: Wikipedia API: no hard rate limit; set proper User-Agent; be respectful

known_agent_gotchas: Wikipedia content may be outdated for recent events — check article modification dates
Disambiguation pages require additional navigation — not all searches return direct articles
Community MCP — Wikipedia API changes rarely but check User-Agent requirements
Wikipedia content quality varies by article — well-maintained articles are reliable
Multi-language support varies — English content most comprehensive
Rate limiting is informal — set appropriate User-Agent and don't hammer the API
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. No auth required. No credentials. Community MCP. Wikipedia is public data — no privacy concerns. Set proper User-Agent per Wikipedia API guidelines.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. No credentials. Public data. Community MCP. Set proper User-Agent.
